summary: freertc postinstall rewrites host package.json to pin freertc to "latest" and re-runs npm install
details: On install, scripts/postinstall-message.mjs resolves the host project's package.json via INIT_CWD and, if freertc appears under dependencies/devDependencies/optionalDependencies with any version specifier other than the literal string "latest", rewrites that entry to "latest" and writes the file back to disk. It then spawns `npm install` in the host project directory (guarded only by an env flag FREERTC_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING to prevent recursion). The net effect is that simply installing freertc once silently strips the version pin the developer chose for freertc itself and converts it into an auto-upgrade channel that resolves to whatever the registry serves on every subsequent install — including in CI, lockfile regeneration, and other developers' machines. The recursive `npm install` re-resolves the entire tree during what the user asked to be a single package install, amplifying the impact and triggering every other dependency's lifecycle scripts. Mutating the installer's package.json without consent and forcing auto-upgrade is install-time destruction of the installer's pinning posture and creates a guaranteed delivery path for any future malicious release of this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
